envelope.interval_scope: research-input sensitivity with dataset anchors (l, n) held fixed

identity.title: All Other Converted Paper Product Manufacturing

lens.customer_and_revenue_model: Repeat unit sales and production runs to food producers, distributors, industrial customers, packers, and private-label buyers under purchase orders, supply agreements, or recurring specifications.
lens.deviation_from_default: The residual NAICS category combines molded-pulp food packaging, industrial converted components, crepe paper, and paper novelties with materially different demand and commercial models. For coherence, the lens retains repeat B2B molded-pulp and specification-driven converted-product suppliers and excludes novelty-led consumer businesses.
lens.excluded_activities: One-off or trend-driven consumer paper novelties and decorative crepe-paper businesses, captive converters, upstream mills, pure distributors, non-control financing vehicles, and owner-dependent craft operations without transferable production and customer relationships.
lens.included_activities: US lower-middle-market converters supplying repeat business-to-business runs of molded-pulp egg cartons, food trays and containers, industrial or protective paper components, and other specification-driven die-cut or converted paper products not classified elsewhere.

a.bridge: BLS measures occupation structure for all NAICS 322 paper manufacturing, not wage-weighted AI task exposure for the narrowed 322299 lens. In May 2023, production occupations were 53.51% of NAICS 322 employment, transportation/material-moving occupations were 12.88%, and installation/maintenance/repair occupations were 8.47%, while office/administrative support was 7.48%, management 5.06%, and business/financial operations 3.22%. I treat the large physical-work share as a constraint and assign most exposure to office, planning, estimating, commercial, design-document, and analytic tasks. Smaller specialty converters may carry more quoting and engineering labor than the broader subsector, so the proxy may understate their exposure.
caveats: The BLS population is NAICS 322, not 322299 or the narrowed lens.
Occupational employment is not itself a measure of task substitutability or current AI adoption.
a.change_evidence: A wage-weighted task-time study from 10-15 independent molded-pulp and specification-driven 322299 plants, including current automation and time by workflow, would materially change the estimate.
a.rationale: Core molding, die-cutting, forming, feeding, packing, material movement, maintenance, and physical inspection remain equipment- and site-dependent. AI can substitute portions of estimating, specification intake, artwork and document checks, scheduling, purchasing, inventory exception handling, sales support, and administration.

caveats: No constant-price, constant-quality forecast exists for the narrowed lens.
EPA waste data are broad, historical, and not a forecast of 322299 demand.
Policy-driven fiber substitution can be offset by material reduction or alternative reusable formats.
d5.change_evidence: Census product-level real shipments and physical-unit data, customer packaging-conversion pipelines, and distributor scanner or procurement volumes for molded pulp and specialty converted components would materially change the estimate.
d5.rationale: Food, egg, produce, protective, and industrial packaging uses recur with underlying shipment volumes, and interest in fiber-based or compostable formats can support substitution from some plastics. Lightweighting, reuse, customer redesign, imports, and the broad category's exposure to declining novelty or discretionary products temper growth.

caveats: The provided n uses a common paper-industry margin bridge despite diverse products.
Narrowing makes eligibility especially sensitive to unknown product mix.
e.change_evidence: A firm-by-firm classification of the 112 dataset-estimated candidates by product family, ownership, customer recurrence, normalized EBITDA, and owner dependence would materially change eligibility.
e.rationale: The residual NAICS code is unusually heterogeneous. Many independent plants do provide repeat B2B converting services, but novelty-led firms, captive operations, non-transferable owner-driven shops, firms outside the true EBITDA band, and businesses without recurring customer programs are excluded.

l.detail: QCEW 2024 private total annual wages at 322299 ($1,180,632,785) x benefits multiplier 1.4294 [ECEC private-industry total comp $46.60/hr / wages $32.60/hr = 1.4294 (March 2026 reference period)] / SUSB 2022 receipts at 322299 ($5,708,419,000). Vintage mismatch: 2024 wages over 2022 receipts, stated. Level: 6-digit. Harmonized to IPS basis ÷1.3407 (median of 65 dual-method codes).

n.detail: margin 0.1465 (margins.json[32]: Damodaran Jan 2026: Paper/Forest Products EBITDA/Sales 14.65% (mid-range for wood/paper/plastics)); revenue/employee $365,035 (SUSB 2022 receipts/employment at 322299); class <5: 74 firms, avg revenue/firm $541,865 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $79,383 -> out of $1-10M band; class 5-9: 35 firms, avg revenue/firm $1,340,629 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $196,402 -> out of $1-10M band; class 10-19: 48 firms, avg revenue/firm $3,609,542 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $528,798 -> out of $1-10M band; class 20-99: 79 firms, avg revenue/firm $14,717,886 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $2,156,170 -> IN $1-10M band; class 100-499: 33 firms, avg revenue/firm $38,241,333 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $5,602,355 -> IN $1-10M band; class 500+: 37 firms, avg revenue/firm $81,715,243 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $11,971,283 -> out of $1-10M band; n_band = 112

caveats: Import displacement can remove a US operator even when end demand persists.
The residual category includes products with different substitution pathways.
o.change_evidence: Customer interviews and lost-order data quantifying vertical integration, imports, digital elimination, reusable-format substitution, and print-on-demand or on-site converting would materially change the estimate.
o.rationale: Demand that remains is for physical molded or converted components and therefore requires accountable tooling, forming or cutting, quality, food-contact suitability where applicable, packaging, and fulfillment. Software may automate ordering and design assistance but cannot wholly self-serve the physical output.

caveats: No source directly measures retention of AI-derived savings.
Capture differs greatly between custom molded components and commodity trays or cartons.
q.change_evidence: Representative contracts, bid records, price-adjustment clauses, customer concentration, quote win/loss data, and gross-margin changes after productivity improvements would establish actual retention.
q.rationale: Unit-priced repeat products permit retention of workflow savings until bids or negotiations reset. Customized tooling, customer qualifications, food-contact performance, short runs, and service reliability reduce immediate comparability, while high-volume commodity programs and concentrated buyers create pass-through pressure.

caveats: This is bounded judgment rather than an observed five-year adoption rate.
Food-contact requirements apply only to part of the narrowed lens.
rho.change_evidence: Plant-level before/after implementations reporting sustained labor hours, quote cycle time, schedule adherence, scrap, specification errors, model exceptions, and user adoption would materially update this range.
rho.rationale: Document-heavy quoting, specification review, planning, purchasing, order entry, customer service, and quality-record workflows can adopt assisted automation within five years. Fragmented ERP data, diverse product specifications, small-firm implementation capacity, food-contact controls, and the need for accountable physical verification limit conversion of exposure into operating results.

caveats: No six-digit, lens-matched control-transfer series was found.
Asset-only plant closures and internal reorganizations must be excluded.
s5.change_evidence: A denominator-matched ten-year set of 322299 control transactions from PitchBook, Capital IQ, GF Data, or Axial, paired with owner-age and succession-plan data for the narrowed candidate universe, would materially change the estimate.
s5.rationale: A fragmented mature specialty-manufacturing population should see retirement, strategic consolidation, and sponsor transactions, but family transfers, failed processes, customer concentration, equipment condition, and product-specific diligence prevent many eligible firms from completing qualifying control sales.

principal_driver: Repeat custom specifications and fragmented quote-to-production workflows create usable opportunities in estimating, order handling, planning, procurement, and quality documentation.
principal_weakness: A residual, heterogeneous NAICS code makes product mix, firm eligibility, demand, and transferability unusually hard to infer from industry-level evidence.
